pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'Create a new folder named "Recovered-Critical"' (line 5 of task.json); setup and export scripts reference this folder name

Verified through email asset analysis: 17 emails from /workspace/assets/emails/ham/ match the pattern (exmh-workers|ilug). Setup script line 44 uses grep pattern '^(List-Id|X-Mailing-List|Subject):.*(exmh-workers|ilug)'. Web search confirms ILUG = Irish Linux Users Group (active mailing list at linux.ie)
Task description explicitly states 'Draft an email to "ops-manager@techcorp.org"' (line 5 of task.json); verifier.py line 31 uses this email from metadata

Setup script analysis (setup_task.sh lines 54-81): Script copies ALL 17 matching target emails to Trash (verified by grepping email assets with pattern '^(List-Id|X-Mailing-List|Subject):.*(exmh-workers|ilug)'). TRASH_TARGET=15 at line 57, but NEEDED=(15-17)=-2 at line 71, so condition at line 72 fails and no padding emails added. Actual trash contains 17 emails, not 15. Script saves actual count to /tmp/initial_trash_count at line 115

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic email recovery task using assets from /workspace/assets/emails/ham (50 emails total, 17 from exmh-workers and ILUG mailing lists). ILUG is the Irish Linux Users Group, a real mailing list community. The task requires recovering deleted emails from a trash folder by identifying emails from the exmh-workers (2 emails) and ILUG (15 emails) mailing lists, creating a recovery folder named 'Recovered-Critical', and confirming completion via email to ops-manager@techcorp.org. CRITICAL DISCREPANCY: The metadata claims initial_trash_count=15, but the setup script would populate trash with all 17 target emails (not padded to 15), making the actual initial trash count 17.
